description_long: This is a futuristic, twin-fuselage airplane with a striking red exterior and a sleek, aerodynamic design. The aircraft features four large, circular jet engines mounted on short pylons, two on each side, and a pair of swept-back wings connecting the twin fuselages. The cockpit is centrally located at the front of each fuselage, with dark, tinted canopies suggesting an enclosed, multi-seat configuration. The tail section includes twin vertical stabilizers and a horizontal stabilizer connecting the two fuselages, forming an H-tail design. The surface is smooth and glossy, with visible panel lines and a registration number 'N7164J' on the side.
description: A sleek, red, twin-fuselage airplane with four large jet engines, swept-back wings, and an H-tail configuration. The cockpit is enclosed and positioned at the front of each fuselage.
description_view_0: Rear view showing the twin vertical stabilizers, horizontal stabilizer, and the large jet engine exhausts.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the twin fuselages, swept-back wings, and the registration number on the side.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the dual cockpits, engine intakes, and the aerodynamic nose sections.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the engine arrangement and tail structure.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing, swept-back wings connecting twin fuselages, no visible winglets, moderate dihedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Four jet engines, two mounted on each side between the fuselages.
attribute_tail_design: H-tail configuration with twin vertical stabilizers and a connecting horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable tricycle landing gear, with main gear under each fuselage and a nose gear under each cockpit.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpits at the front of each fuselage, likely multi-seat with dark canopies.
